title: Sunroom Loveseat
summary: Turn your sunroom or porch into a comfortable place to hang out with this love seat.
description: Turn your sunroom or porch into a comfortable place to hang out with this love seat. With indoor/outdoor cushions, it’s perfect for a covered porch or patio, or for a three-season porch. Plus, you can build it easily using one sheet of plywood, a few boards, and a set of off-the-shelf cushion.

steps.title: Make a Pair of Side Panels
steps.body: Cut two Side Panels from 3/4" plywood, as shown in the cutting diagram. The easiest way to do this is to cut a long rectangle first, then lay out the angles, and cut the pieces to final shape.

steps.title: Make the Back Panel
steps.body: Cut the Back Panel to size from 3/4" plywood, as shown in the cutting diagram. Then, with your pocket hole jig set up for 3/4" material, drill pocket holes where shown.

steps.title: Attach the Back to the Sides
steps.body: Attach the Side Panels to the Back Panel using 1 1/4" exterior pocket holes screws, as shown.

steps.title: Make a Seat Panel
steps.body: Cut the Seat Panel to size from 3/4" plywood, as shown on the cutting diagram. With your pocket hole jig set up for 3/4" material, drill pocket holes where shown.

steps.title: Attach the Seat Panel
steps.body: Lay the Side/Back assembly on its back. Secure the Seat Panel using 1 1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Create a Pair of Arms
steps.body: Cut two arms from a 1 x 2 board, as shown in the cutting diagram. Then attach the Arms to the Side Panels, as shown, using exterior-rated wood glue and brad nails.

steps.title: Make Legs and Side Rails
steps.body: Cut four Legs and to length from a 2 x 2 board, as shown in the cutting diagram, and then set them aside. Then cut four Side Rails to length. With your pocket hole jig set up for 1 1/2" material, drill pocket holes where shown in the Side Rails.

steps.title: Make Front and Back Rails
steps.body: Cut four Front/Back Rails to length from a 2 x 2 board, as shown in the cutting diagram. With your pocket hole jig set for 1 1/2" thick material, drill pocket holes where shown in the Front/Back Rails.

steps.title: Assemble the Base
steps.body: Attach the Side Rails to the Legs with 2 1/2" exterior pocket hole screws to create two assemblies. Then join these assemblies by attaching the Front and Back Rails using 2 1/2" exterior pocket hole screws. Note the pocket holes are all on the same side of the base parts. This will be the bottom.

steps.title: Paint and Do Final Assembly
steps.body: Sand and paint the Base and the Seat separately then secure the base to the Seat with glue and 2" wood screws. Use two screws through each upper Front/Back Rail, and one through each upper Side Rail. Drill pilot holes through the Rails before you drive the screws.
